NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A FACTUAL BASIS FOR LIABILITY AGAINST CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFISIA INVESTMENT & REALTY CO. ("PACIFISIA"). WHILE THE PARTIES LARGELY BASE THEIR ARGUMENTS ON THE VIABILITY OF CLAIMS AGAINST SUCCESSIVE VERSUS CONCURRENT TORTFEASORS, THESE ARGUMENTS DO NOT REFER TO ALLEGATIONS ON THE FACE OF THE CROSS COMPLAINT. THE ONLY SPECIFIC FACT ALLEGED IN THE CROSS COMPLAINT REGARDING PACIFISIA IS THAT IT IS A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY DOING BUSINESS IN SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA. ALTHOUGH THE CROSS COMPLAINT INCORPORATES THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT BY REFERENCE, PACIFISIA IS NOT IDENTIFIED ANYWHERE IN THE COMPLAINT EITHER. THE CROSS COMPLAINT DOES NOT INCORPORATE ANY OTHER PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. Off Calendar Oct-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT PACIFISIA INVESTMENT & REALTY CO.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN CROSS-COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, with leave to amend to allege a factual basis for liability against Cross Defendant Pacifisia Investment & Realty Co. ("Pacifisia"). While the parties largely base their arguments on the viability of claims against successive versus concurrent tortfeasors, these arguments do not refer to allegations on the face of the Cross Complaint. The only specific fact alleged in the Cross Complaint regarding Pacifisia is that it is a California Limited Liability Company doing business in San Francisco, California. Although the Cross Complaint incorporates the allegations of the Complaint by reference, Pacifisia is not identified anywhere in the Complaint either. The Cross Complaint does not incorporate any other pleadings. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC, ANDREW NICHOLLS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-604855, CGC-23-608547, CGC-23-607017 WITH CASE CGC-22-603644.

Defendants' (D2 PROPERTIES OF FLORIDA LLC, ANDREW NICHOLLS) motion to consolidate is granted in part and denied in part.

The court consolidates the following cases for trial purposes: CGC-22-603644, CGC-23-604855, and CGC-23-607017. These cases involve common issues of law and fact regarding the alleged habitability issues at the properties located at 244-246 Divisadero Street and 877-881 Haight Street. The court deems the trial date in CGC-23-607017 (July 28, 2025) as the controlling trial date. Any party is free to seek a different trial date before the presiding judge in department 206.

The motion to consolidate is denied as to case CGC-23-608547. This case involves a neighboring property. While there is some overlap with the other actions, a large part of the case relates to how a different landlord (Felink) responding to the fire, which implicates different rights and responsibilities.

The parties are ordered to coordinate discovery and motion practice to promote efficiency and avoid duplicative proceedings.

Counsel for the moving party, D2 Properties of Florida LLC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
